content.full_text: UMC, HyperLight to mass-produce TFLN photonics chiplets for AI data centers United Microelectronics Corporation (UMC) has partnered with startup HyperLight to mass-produce thin-film lithium niobate (TFLN) chiplets, aiming to improve data transmission speeds in artificial intelligence computing clusters and data centers. title: From digital access to learning outcomes: India’s edtech emphasis needs to evolve
author: Amitabh Kant, Gouri Gupta

content.full_text: From digital access to learning outcomes: India’s edtech emphasis needs to evolve The Bharat Survey for EdTech shows that digital access is now widespread in India, but technologies aren’t fully understood. The focus of edtech must shift from access to structured learning and improving outcomes to deliver the human capital gains we need fo… This ambition rests on human capital. Sustained economic growth will depend not only on infrastructure and innovation, but on workforce productivity. At its foundation, this means ensuring that ever…

ai_output.summary: A survey in India has found that digital access to education is now widespread, but understanding of technology in education is limited. The focus of education technology (edtech) should shift from just providing access to improving learning outcomes. This is necessary for India to achieve human capital gains, which are essential for sustained economic growth. Economic growth also relies on workforce productivity. The government needs to prioritize improving learning outcomes.

title: 'Elon Musk Unveiled: The Tesla Experiment': When unchecked ambition costs lives
author: Elizabeth Grenier

content.full_text: 'Elon Musk Unveiled: The Tesla Experiment': When unchecked ambition costs lives A documentary explores how Tesla drivers serve as unpaid beta testers of the company's self-driving cars, while Elon Musk focuses on his long-term goal: colonizing Mars. Elon Musk, who has topped for the second year in a row Forbes' Billionaires list publishe… Elon Musk, who has topped for the second year in a row Forbes' Billionaires list published on March 10, is on course to become the world's first trillionaire. On top of his extreme wealth, Musk is b…

ai_output.summary: A documentary titled "Elon Musk Unveiled: The Tesla Experiment" explores how Tesla drivers are used as unpaid beta testers for the company's self-driving cars. The drivers are not being paid to test the cars, which can put their lives at risk. Meanwhile, Elon Musk is focused on his long-term goal of colonizing Mars. He has topped Forbes' Billionaires list for the second year in a row. Musk is on track to become the world's first trillionaire, according to Forbes. His extreme wealth and ambitious goals are the subject of the documentary.

title: Elon Musk's Tesla experiment: Unchecked ambition costs lives
author: Elizabeth Grenier

content.full_text: Elon Musk's Tesla experiment: Unchecked ambition costs lives A documentary explores how Tesla drivers serve as unpaid beta testers of the company's self-driving cars while Elon Musk focuses on his long-term goal: colonizing Mars. Elon Musk, who this week topped Forbes' Billionaires list for the second year in a row, is on course to become the world's first trillionaire. On top of his extreme wealth, Musk is bound to remain i…

ai_output.summary: A documentary critiques Elon Musk's approach to testing Tesla's self-driving cars, calling it an "unchecked ambition" that costs lives. Drivers of Tesla vehicles are essentially unpaid beta testers, allowing the company to gather data for its autonomous vehicles. The documentary questions whether the pursuit of innovation is worth the risks to human life. Elon Musk's focus on colonizing Mars is highlighted as an example of his long-term vision. Musk's wealth is noted as a key factor in his ability to pursue such ambitious goals.

title: Social media giants have grand plans for AI. Creators fear they'll be left out.
author: Sydney Bradley,Dan Whateley

content.full_text:
Social media giants have grand plans for AI. Creators fear they'll be left out. Social apps like Instagram and TikTok have their attention set on AI, and it's making some creators feel like an afterthought. Getty Images; Tyler Le/BI
  • Influencers are reckoning with a new boogeyman: artificial intelligence.
  • Social apps seem infatuated with AI, and it's making some creators feel like an aft…

ai_output.summary: Social media companies such as Instagram and TikTok are investing heavily in AI technology. This has raised concerns among content creators that they will be left behind. Influencers are now worried about the impact of AI on their livelihoods. The increased focus on AI has created a sense of uncertainty among creators who fear they will no longer be needed.

Therefore, owners must assist with bathing and other hygiene practices. Understanding Ferr… While ferrets engage in some self-grooming, they do not completely wash themselves like cats. Therefore, owners must assist with bathing and other hygiene practices. Understanding Ferret Grooming Ha…

ai_output.summary: Ferrets engage in some self-grooming, but they do not completely wash themselves like cats. They require assistance with bathing and other hygiene practices from their owners. Ferrets do not have the ability to completely clean themselves like some other animals. As a result, owners must take on a role in keeping their ferrets clean. This includes bathing and other hygiene practices.

title: Semco seen supplying ABF substrates for Tesla AI chips via Samsung foundry
author: DIGITIMES

content.full_text: Semco seen supplying ABF substrates for Tesla AI chips via Samsung foundry Samsung Electro-Mechanics (Semco) is expected to supply ABF substrates for Tesla's next-generation artificial intelligence chips that Samsung Electronics plans to manufacture at its Taylor, Texas foundry, according to Chosun Biz and other Korean media reports. The partnership highlights the growing demand for advanced semiconductor components in the AI industry.

title: Monopoly fears rise as Dangote controls N14.4tn petrol market
author: Punch Newspapers

content.full_text: Monopoly fears rise as Dangote controls N14.4tn petrol market Experts raise alarms over monopoly risks as the Dangote refinery dominates Nigeria’s N14.4tn petrol market, urging price regulation and competition. Read More: https://punchng.com/monopoly-fears-rise-as-dangote-controls-n14-4tn-petrol-market/ Stakeholders, including energy experts, economists, and Nigerian workers, have raised alarm over the suspension of petrol imports by the Federal Government, urging urgent price regulation as Dangote …

ai_output.summary: Nigeria's petrol market, valued at N14.4tn, has raised concerns about monopoly control by Dangote's refinery. Experts are urging the government to regulate prices and promote competition. The suspension of petrol imports by the government has heightened these concerns. Stakeholders, including energy experts and economists, are calling for urgent action. The dominant market position of Dangote's refinery has sparked fears of a monopoly.

title: Early, contrarian AI trend has global IT firms hiring more in India to deliver growth, improve margins
author: Jas Bardia, Varun Sood

content.full_text: Early, contrarian AI trend has global IT firms hiring more in India to deliver growth, improve margins Capgemini, Cognizant, and EPAM are increasing India headcount to leverage local talent as demand for tech workers rises due to AI integration. This trend, driven by a need to protect margins, contrasts with some Indian peers reducing workforce. Read the trend… Analysts see this as the beginning of a new trend as artificial intelligence (AI)-based technologies increasingly become part of IT services workflows and give rise to demand for tech workers to moni…

ai_output.summary: Several global IT firms, including Capgemini, Cognizant, and EPAM, are increasing their headcount in India to take advantage of local talent and growing demand for tech workers. This trend is driven by the integration of artificial intelligence (AI) technologies into IT services workflows. Analysts believe this is a new trend that will continue as AI becomes more prevalent. The move is also aimed at protecting margins as companies adapt to changing market conditions. Some Indian peers are reducing their workforce, but these global firms are bucking this trend.
